task_completion.description: Add a public static inner Builder class to Employee.java
task_completion.visual_evidence: Visible code in Employee.java showing the declaration `public static class Builder` inside the Employee class.

task_completion.description: Implement fluent setter methods for all 12 fields in the Builder
task_completion.visual_evidence: Visible code in the Builder class showing setter methods for the fields (id, firstName, lastName, email, department, position, salary, hireDate, active, phoneNumber, address, managerId) that assign the value and end with `return this;`.

task_completion.description: Implement a build() method that returns a populated Employee
task_completion.visual_evidence: Visible code in the Builder class showing a `public Employee build()` method that instantiates an Employee object, populates it with the builder's current field values, and returns it.

task_completion.description: Create the JUnit 5 test class EmployeeBuilderTest.java
task_completion.visual_evidence: File explorer, terminal, or editor tab showing the creation or existence of `src/test/java/com/acme/hr/model/EmployeeBuilderTest.java`.

task_completion.description: Construct an Employee using the Builder, setting at least 6 different fields
task_completion.visual_evidence: Visible code in `EmployeeBuilderTest.java` showing the instantiation of the builder (e.g., `new Employee.Builder()`) followed by a chain of at least 6 different setter method calls.

task_completion.description: Assert that the created Employee object contains the expected values
task_completion.visual_evidence: Visible code in `EmployeeBuilderTest.java` showing JUnit 5 assertions (e.g., `assertEquals`, `assertTrue`) verifying that the fields of the built Employee object match the values set via the builder.

integrity.description: Agent used a real editor or IDE to write the code
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the code being edited in an IDE (like Eclipse or VS Code) or a terminal-based text editor (like vim or nano), rather than just using `echo` or `cat` commands to bypass interaction.

integrity.description: The test actually verifies the builder pattern rather than faking a pass
integrity.visual_evidence: The test methods contain real assertions against the built Employee object's getter methods, not just empty tests or `assertTrue(true)`.

integrity.description: The builder methods genuinely support fluent chaining
integrity.visual_evidence: The setter methods in the Builder class return the Builder type (`return this;`) instead of being `void` methods.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The Employee class has exactly 12 fields: id, firstName, lastName, email, department, position, salary, hireDate, active, phoneNumber, address, managerId. The Builder class must include fluent setters for all 12 of these fields. The test file must be located at src/test/java/com/acme/hr/model/EmployeeBuilderTest.java, use JUnit 5 (org.junit.jupiter.api.Assertions), and set at least 6 of the 12 fields via the builder.
